title: Nacibea racemosa
taxonomy: Nacibea racemosa is a species within the flowering plant family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This classification places Nacibea racemosa alongside other species that share similar characteristics, such as fruit and flower morphology. The Annonaceae family comprises approximately 200 genera and over 2,000 species. Nacibea racemosa is a part of the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The exact classification of Nacibea racemosa may vary depending on the source and any recent taxonomic revisions.

intro: Nacibea racemosa, also known as Guanabanera, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Nacibea racemosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and are also valued for their potential to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
